Now you have enough customer commonality in many cases that you can sort of semi-standardize that. So if I'm in Barnes & Noble and I see a really cool book about magic that I didn't see before, didn't know it exists, right? I immediately think, well, hey, this would be this David like this. So I'll get that, put it in the closet for the next time I need to save Dave something. But there's at least four or five other clients I got who this will work for. But they'll think I picked it for them. So I can at least do five. And so you maneuver in between as to the size and the magnitude of the gift. It's like ball tickets or season tickets. Your job as a entrepreneur is to go figure out how you can do it. Okay. So registered investment advisors are theoretically prohibited from using referrals.
